oneLiner: PEP is a light, refreshing protein drink for modern lifestyles — wellness without sacrifice, made for everyday moments.
positioningStatement: PEP enables people to enjoy healthier, feel-good choices in their daily lives. It's a refreshing, protein-boosted drink that fits right into brunches, sunny afternoons, coworking sessions or moments of relaxation — all with zero gym vibes.
audience: People who want healthier routines without effort
occasions: Refreshing after yoga or Pilates (not heavy workouts)
Non-drink occasions: book shops, galleries, wellness retail

voice.tone: Modern, upbeat, approachable, and gently inspiring. Never clinical or 'gym-protein'. Copy should feel like a warm friend inviting you out.

visual.mood: Minimal, pastel, airy and premium-without-bling. Never shouty or hyperactive. Emphasize lightness, balance, and freshness.
visual.photography: Bright, natural lighting; soft casual scenes (brunch tables, magazines, sun, greens); people in relaxed, aspirational but everyday settings. No intense gym shots. Focus on mood and occasion.
visual.typography: Elegant and clean — lead with Didot for main wordmark, paired with Montserrat for clarity on labels; Brittany Signature for script elements like the tagline. Avoid heavy sans-serifs or 'action' fonts.
rejectLooks: Cluttered or overly technical label layouts

inLineBrands.name: BLOOM
inLineBrands.why: Integrates gut health and prebiotic benefits with playful, lively, and wellness-driven branding — directly targets health-conscious, lifestyle-focused consumers.

inLineBrands.name: EVER TREE
inLineBrands.why: Minimalist, elegant design; naturally refreshing taste, positioned for mindful and healthier everyday choices.

inLineBrands.name: OLIPOP
inLineBrands.why: Soft, friendly branding; positions as a healthy lifestyle soda for wellness-aware audiences.

inLineBrands.name: Poppi
inLineBrands.why: Wellness-forward, vibrant branding with strong health cues — appeals to young, social, fun-loving market looking for feel-good beverages.

inLineBrands.name: Alive & Well
inLineBrands.why: Balances modern wellness focus with unique, smooth taste and natural, organic positioning; avoids overly 'fitness' messaging.

inLineBrands.name: Recess
inLineBrands.why: Minimalist, pastel visual style; targets relaxation and healthy lifestyle occasions; playful, modern copy and photography.

inLineBrands.name: Ole cocktails
inLineBrands.why: Premium, modern, and vibrant design — appeals to inclusive, lifestyle/social moments with real ingredient focus.

inLineBrands.name: Ish
inLineBrands.why: Minimalist Scandinavian layout, premium yet approachable; positioned as a lifestyle upgrade for social and wellness-minded consumers.

inLineBrands.name: Yaya Kombucha
inLineBrands.why: Strong authenticity and health credentials, modern and clean design with bold flavor cues, fits daily wellness and casual refreshment occasions.
designPatterns: Clean, modern label with artisan touches

inLineBrands.name: Walker Brothers
inLineBrands.why: Natural, craft positioning draws on nuance and authentic botanical inspiration; well-executed brand for everyday sophistication.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Functional protein/hydration lane: vitamin content and clinical health focus lacks lifestyle context/fun; not relevant for mindful, everyday lifestyle moments.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Sports/electrolyte targeted for gym/nutrition routines; packaging feels medical versus aspirational lifestyle.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Sports energy and BCAA focus for athletic use, with bold, gym-driven visuals — not social or wellness lifestyle oriented.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Niche protein-alcohol hybrid; skews experimental and gym-adjacent — misses broader, everyday lifestyle appeal.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Practical vitamin/hydration solution with functional design; lacks emotional, aspirational brand personality fitting lifestyle uplift.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Kin Euphorics
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Explicitly social/experiential, mood-enhancing for nightlife/party occasions — more thrill-seeking, less everyday wellness.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Lyre's
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Alcohol-free spirit for cocktail mimicry; visual cues and ritual lean hard into nightlife/bar as opposed to daily balance.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Seedlip
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Ultra-premium, social-drink alternative — bar/ritual focused, less fitting for everyday lifestyle snacking or refreshment moments.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body, gym-focused protein RTDs
antiReferences.why: Pure muscle-building, fitness-protein positioning and 'shaker bottle' cues are completely off-brand for PEP's relaxed, lifestyle intent.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com (US, probiotic/energy)
antiReferences.why: Name collision risk and different market: probiotic/energy brand with functional/energy focus — not same mood or context.

antiReferences.name: Generic sports/energy drinks
antiReferences.why: Overly energetic, techy/clinical, and performance-led. Lacks warmth, balance, or casual lifestyle appeal.

antiReferences.name: Any product with bodybuilding/body-composition transformation as hero
antiReferences.why: Enforces intimidating or exclusive goals, loses everyday enjoyment and inclusivity.

brandFitRules: Ignore brands whose primary context is gym, workout recovery, or muscle gain.
Prioritize brands/drinks that support casual, daily wellness or mindful lifestyle upgrade moments.
Look for brands designed for consumption in social, leisure, or relaxation settings outside the gym.
Avoid visuals with bold, clinical, or techy fonts/colors (e.g., neon, metallic, 'action' palettes).
Reject any reference that uses aggressive, high-gloss, or competitive sports imagery.
Do not use words that invoke hardcore nutrition, athletic optimization, or energy-shot vibes.
Copy and design should feel accessible, warm, light, and slightly playful — but still premium.
Photography must emphasize relaxed, aspirational everyday moments (brunch, coworking, leisure).
